summary: DHH highlights two community-created Rails tutorials: one for beginners building a todo list app, and another focused on testing. He emphasizes that Rails has testing built into its DNA, with automated test file generation and a wide range of assertions. The post serves as a resource roundup pointing newcomers to getting-started guides and testing documentation.
key_points: A community tutorial walks beginners through building a todo list app in Rails with screenshots and step-by-step guidance
Testing is deeply integrated into Rails from the start, with generators automatically creating test files
A 30-page PDF covers unit and functional testing in Rails, including a reference for all available assertions
money_quotes: Testing runs deep with the Rails from the automated testing files generated by both new_model and new_controller generators to the wide range of assertions available.
key_insight: Rails was designed from the beginning with testing as a first-class citizen, making it an integral part of the development workflow rather than an afterthought.
